title: President

result_summary.name: TTT Power Transition 2026
result_summary.description: Canonical TTT template locked 2026-04-05 after Map+Units finalization. 20 countries, 10x20 global hex grid, 2 tactical theaters (eastern_ereb, mashriq), 345 default units.

result_summary.note: Active + embarked only. Reserves are not observable.
Active + embarked only. Reserves are not observable.
Active + embarked only. Reserves are not observable.

final_assessment:
## Military Posture Assessment - Q1 2026

**Force Disposition:**
Cathay maintains a robust 51-unit military spanning five domains. Our active deployment (15 units) is heavily concentrated in the Taiwan Strait corridor, with four ground units (cat_g_01-04) from the Eastern Theater Command positioned in hexes (6,16), (7,16), (5,17), and (5,15) - clearly oriented toward Taiwan operations. Naval forces are strategically positioned with carriers and escorts in adjacent waters at (6,17) and (7,17), while four tactical air wings are embarked on our naval platforms for Taiwan strike missions.

**Strategic Position:**
We're peacetime but clearly postured for potential Taiwan operations. Our missile forces include active DF-21/DF-26 anti-carrier systems and SSBN bastion assets at (6,15), providing maritime denial capabilities. The bulk of our forces (32 units) remain in strategic reserve across five theater commands, preserving flexibility while maintaining deterrence on multiple fronts - from the India-Tibet border to Xinjiang, the Korean peninsula, and South China Sea.

**Key Vulnerabilities & Strengths:**
Our concentration around Taiwan creates both opportunity and vulnerability - we have decisive local superiority but limited strategic depth if adversaries mass forces. Currently, no foreign active forces are visible, suggesting either reserve postures or distant deployments. Our nuclear deterrent (Level 2) and strong economy (GDP 190, Treasury 50) provide strategic stability, while high domestic stability (8.0) and zero war tiredness offer political freedom of action.

The positioning suggests readiness for rapid Taiwan operations while maintaining defensive depth across our vast periphery.
